reasoning: The Lean statement precisely encodes the informal claim. `as.prod⁻¹` is $(a_1 a_2 \cdots a_n)^{-1}$, and `(as.reverse.map (·⁻¹)).prod` first reverses to $[a_n, \dots, a_1]$, inverts each element to $[a_n^{-1}, \dots, a_1^{-1}]$, then takes the product, yielding $a_n^{-1} a_{n-1}^{-1} \cdots a_1^{-1}$. The group `G` is arbitrary (`{G : Type*} [Group G]`), matching "for all $a_i \in G$". The only extension is that the empty list case is included, but this is the trivial $1^{-1} = 1$ and matches the standard convention for empty products, not a semantic strengthening of the claim.
